  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Section advances an explicit contribution claim (4.2 NDCG@5 gain, new SOTA) and frames a conceptual thesis (contrastive answer-prediction as neural information-gain); coherence of that framing must be verified.",
    "R1.2": "Load-bearing: the final paragraph concludes both components are 'essential' and that results 'substantially advance SOTA,' but the only comparisons are against two self-ablations — no independent prior-work baseline appears, creating a logical gap between evidence and conclusion.",
    "R1.3": "Load-bearing: the SOTA claim and the '4.2 NDCG@5' headline are the section's empirical core; the numbers internally check out (0.612−0.570=0.042), but the claim that this beats 'prior work' is unsupported by any comparison to an external system.",
    "R1.4": "The contrastive objective is presented as novel, and prior work is invoked by name ('inquiry modeling,' 'expected-information-gain') but never cited or scored against; the novelty delta over the nearest real competitor is asserted, not demonstrated.",
    "R1.6": "Standard review expectation; prose is competent but the final sentence ('confirms… are essential ingredients') overstates what ablations can show and warrants a rewrite regardless of review scope.",
    "R1.7": "Multiple appeals to 'prior work' and the StackExchange-Tex benchmark appear without any citation in this excerpt; reference coverage and attribution must be checked against the full paper."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should this review focus primarily on scientific rigor — logical consistency, evidence–claim alignment, and novelty over independent prior systems — or primarily on writing quality — prose clarity, style, and sentence-level polish?"
}

rationale.R1.1: Section advances an explicit contribution claim (4.2 NDCG@5 gain, new SOTA) and frames a conceptual thesis (contrastive answer-prediction as neural information-gain); coherence of that framing must be verified.
rationale.R1.2: Load-bearing: the final paragraph concludes both components are 'essential' and that results 'substantially advance SOTA,' but the only comparisons are against two self-ablations — no independent prior-work baseline appears, creating a logical gap between evidence and conclusion.
rationale.R1.3: Load-bearing: the SOTA claim and the '4.2 NDCG@5' headline are the section's empirical core; the numbers internally check out (0.612−0.570=0.042), but the claim that this beats 'prior work' is unsupported by any comparison to an external system.
rationale.R1.4: The contrastive objective is presented as novel, and prior work is invoked by name ('inquiry modeling,' 'expected-information-gain') but never cited or scored against; the novelty delta over the nearest real competitor is asserted, not demonstrated.
rationale.R1.6: Standard review expectation; prose is competent but the final sentence ('confirms… are essential ingredients') overstates what ablations can show and warrants a rewrite regardless of review scope.
rationale.R1.7: Multiple appeals to 'prior work' and the StackExchange-Tex benchmark appear without any citation in this excerpt; reference coverage and attribution must be checked against the full paper.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review focus primarily on scientific rigor — logical consistency, evidence–claim alignment, and novelty over independent prior systems — or primarily on writing quality — prose clarity, style, and sentence-level polish?
